bayesroc 0.1.0
  • Reference
    • Analyzing univariate recognition data with bayesroc
  • Changelog

Rotello et al. (2005) Remember/Know data

rotello_2005.Rd

Aggregated count data for Experiment 1 from Rotello et al. (2005). 48 participants were equally split across two groups (conservative and neutral recollection criterion). Participants studied 60 items and were tested on the same items presented alongside 60 lures. Participants gave 6-point confidence judgements, wherein any judgement > 1 constituted an "old" response. For all "old" responses, participants also provided remember/know judgements.

Usage

rotello_2005

Format

A data frame with 44 rows and 5 columns:

is_old

integer, 0 = lure (new), 1 = studied (old). Use as the is_old indicator in formulas.

rating

integer 1-6 confidence rating (1 = "new", 6 = highest-confidence "old").

rk

the remember/know/new judgement: "remember" or "know" for "old" responses (rating >= 2), "new" for "new" responses (rating == 1).

count

integer count of responses in this cell, summed across participants.

condition

factor with levels "conservative" and "neutral" – the response-bias instruction condition.

Source

Rotello, C. M., Macmillan, N. A., Reeder, J. A., & Wong, M. (2005). The remember response: Subject to bias, graded, and not a process-pure indicator of recollection. Psychonomic Bulletin & Review, 12(5), 865-873. https://doi.org/10.3758/BF03196778
